headline: Shares of construction materials and engineering-related companies are trading higher after Iran signaled that the Strait of Hormuz will remain open for the duration of the ceasefire, easing concerns inflation risk and spurring expectations for monetary easing.
summary: Shares of construction materials and engineering-related companies are trading higher after Iran signaled that the Strait of Hormuz will remain open for the duration of the ceasefire, easing concerns inflation risk and spurring expectations for monetary easing.

raw.headline: Shares of construction materials and engineering-related companies are trading higher after Iran signaled that the Strait of Hormuz will remain open for the duration of the ceasefire, easing concerns inflation risk and spurring expectations for monetary easing.

headline: Shares of household durable stocks are trading higher amid overall market strength after Iran opened the Strait of Hormuz for the remainder of the ceasefire following a 10-day armistice between Israel and Lebanon. This alleviates the ongoing supply disruption and lessens energy costs.
summary: Shares of household durable stocks are trading higher amid overall market strength after Iran opened the Strait of Hormuz for the remainder of the ceasefire following a 10-day armistice between Israel and Lebanon. This alleviates the ongoing supply disruption and lessens energy costs.
